description: 5G connection quality monitoring — latency, jitter, packet loss, and uptime

panels.title: Current Status
panels.description: Live connection status based on the most recent probe

panels.title: Latency (1h avg)
panels.description: Average ping RTT to 8.8.8.8 over the last hour. The Latency Golden Signal — high values mean the link is slow regardless of throughput.

panels.title: Uptime (range %)
panels.description: Percentage of time the link reported connected=1 over the dashboard time range. The Traffic Golden Signal. Replaces the broken Connection Uptime panel — uses avg_over_time directly so sub-5-min outages aren't masked.

panels.title: Packet Loss (1h max)
panels.description: Highest packet-loss percentage to Google or Cloudflare over the last hour. Excludes gateway loss (often legitimately high on cellular). The Errors Golden Signal.

panels.title: Deployed Version
panels.description: Currently deployed version (short-hash). Backed by the towerwatch_build_info Prom gauge, re-emitted every tick — always shows the running version regardless of lookback window.

panels.title: Packet Loss
panels.description: Percentage of TCP probes that failed per 30-second cycle

panels.title: Bufferbloat (latency under load)
panels.description: RTT idle vs. under download/upload load, plus the deltas. A large delta means bufferbloat (our own traffic inflating latency); a flat delta with bad absolute latency points at the carrier.

panels.title: DNS Resolution Time
panels.description: DNS query time per nameserver (bypasses local cache). Pi/M6 only on phone sessions; the phone's DNS timing is in the Phone HTTP Waterfall panel (phone_dns_ms), a different method.

panels.title: TCP Connection Time
panels.description: TCP handshake time. M6/Pi: tcp_connect_ms to 8.8.8.8:443. Phone (standstill-phone): phone_rtt_ms = mean TCP-connect to 8.8.8.8 + 1.1.1.1:443, with phone_jitter_ms (stddev). Same method (TCP connect), so they overlay here; the phone series is tagged host=standstill-phone.

panels.title: HTTP Latency Probe
panels.description: Timed 10KB download from Cloudflare CDN (every 5 min). The phone overlays this panel via the unprefixed http_latency_ms field — pick standstill-phone to see the phone series.

panels.title: Gateway Health
panels.description: TCP connect and HTTP response time to the local gateway. Works on any router — always populated regardless of vendor.

panels.title: Saturation Down (6h)
panels.description: 6-hour average HTTP throughput as a percentage of the plan capacity ($max_download_mbps). 30–80% is healthy active use; >95% means the link is saturated; near 0% means light usage. The Saturation Golden Signal.

panels.title: Saturation Up (6h)
panels.description: Upload utilization as % of configured max.

panels.title: Speedtest Data (7d)
panels.description: Total bytes consumed by speedtests (scheduled + manual) over the past 7 days. Budget reference: 30 GB/month ≈ 7 GB/week.

panels.title: Avg Download Speed
panels.description: Average across both scheduled Cloudflare runs and manual speedtests, over dashboard time range.

panels.title: Avg Upload Speed
panels.description: Average across both scheduled Cloudflare runs and manual speedtests, over dashboard time range.

panels.title: Speedtest History (Cloudflare)
panels.description: Cloudflare adaptive throughput probe — both scheduled and SSH-triggered manual runs. Manual runs are labeled with the operator that triggered them.

panels.title: Daily Avg Download Throughput (14d)
panels.description: One bar per calendar day, 14-day fixed window: the daily mean of Cloudflare speedtest download runs (scheduled + manual). Both the Pi and the phone emit speedtest_download_mbps, so this populates on every site. Sparse until enough runs accumulate. Time range is panel-fixed — does not follow the dashboard time picker.

panels.title: Daily Avg Upload Throughput (14d)
panels.description: One bar per calendar day, 14-day fixed window: the daily mean of Cloudflare speedtest upload runs (scheduled + manual). Both the Pi and the phone emit speedtest_upload_mbps, so this populates on every site. Sparse until enough runs accumulate. Time range is panel-fixed — does not follow the dashboard time picker.

panels.title: M6 Signal Quality
panels.description: Nighthawk M6 signal metrics: RSRP, RSRQ, SINR

panels.title: M6 Carrier Aggregation
panels.description: Aggregated downlink spectrum + component carriers, and the antenna GATE: SCC declared (offered by the tower) vs active (held by the device). declared > active = the tower offers carriers the device cannot hold (signal-limited, antenna may help); declared = active = 1 = no extra spectrum on offer, so an antenna gives ~0 throughput upside. A drop in aggregated bandwidth/carrier count is a throughput cliff.

panels.title: M6 Handover History (PCI / band / eNB)
panels.description: Serving-cell identifiers as state bands: each row holds steady while on a cell and changes colour at a handover or reselection. Each distinct value gets its own colour (not a good/bad gradient), so frequent flips stand out. Watch for constant PCI/eNB flapping correlating with latency or throughput dips.

panels.title: M6 Device Temperature
panels.description: Numeric chassis temperature from the M6 (°C). The prime suspect for afternoon thermal throttling — watch it climb in a sunny window.

panels.title: M6 Current Cell
panels.description: Current serving-cell identity and signal — what cell/band the M6 is on right now. Plain identifiers; no good/bad coloring (these are IDs, not magnitudes).

panels.title: Band Performance Ranking
panels.description: Average signal per serving band over the dashboard range, from the bands the M6 naturally roams across (it can't be force-locked). Samples = dwell time on that band (more samples = more time spent there). Sort by SINR to see which band performed best.

panels.title: Uplink Effort — M6 Tx Level (lower is better)
panels.description: M6 transmit power level. Counterintuitive success signal: you want this to DROP. If the modem transmits near max to reach the tower, a better antenna lets it reach the same tower with less power — a falling tx_level after an antenna change is direct evidence the uplink path improved (and explains any upload gain). Grouped by experiment when a run is active.

panels.title: Latency Tails (p95 + worst) — per target
panels.description: 95th-percentile and worst-case RTT over the dashboard range, per target. Tails matter more than means for A/B comparison — the win is fixing the worst moments, not the average. Pure analysis on existing ping data; no extra cost. Pair with the $location and time picker to scope an experiment window.

panels.title: Service Restarts
panels.description: All service restarts (planned deploys and unplanned crashes) in the selected time range.

panels.title: Phone Serving LTE Signal
panels.description: Pixel serving-cell LTE metrics from the towerwatch-rflogger Android app: RSRP, RSRQ, SINR. Fixed host=standstill-phone (does NOT follow the $location picker). Session-based — gaps mean no logging session was running.

panels.title: Phone NR Signal (5G)
panels.description: Pixel 5G NR signal — the M6's blind spot (its firmware can't expose NR). Populated only while the phone is camped on 5G NR; flat/absent means the phone is on LTE. host=standstill-phone (fixed).

panels.title: Phone Neighbor Cells
panels.description: Aggregated neighbor-cell signal seen by the phone (requires location services on the device). Neighbor count is how many cells are visible besides the serving cell; max/min RSRP bound their signal. Per-PCI series are intentionally NOT emitted (cardinality). host=standstill-phone (fixed).

panels.title: Phone CA Carriers
panels.description: Carrier-aggregation component-carrier count held by the phone (from PhysicalChannelConfig). Higher = more aggregated spectrum. Compare against the M6's carrier count to see if the phone holds more carriers on the same tower. host=standstill-phone (fixed). Sparse/blank = few samples in range (short logging session) or the phone is on LTE; expected, not an error.

panels.title: Phone Band Ranking
panels.description: Average signal per serving band the phone roamed across over the dashboard range. Samples = dwell time on that band. Sort by SINR to see which band performed best for the phone; compare against the M6 Band Performance Ranking for the same window. host=standstill-phone (fixed). Sparse/blank = few samples in range (short logging session); expected, not an error.

panels.title: Phone HTTP Waterfall (DNS · connect · TLS · TTFB)
panels.description: Cold-connection setup breakdown from the rflogger waterfall probe. Lines overlay (not stacked): TTFB is the full first-byte total; DNS / Connect / TLS are components inside it (Connect already includes TLS). Phone-only (no M6 equivalent); phone_dns_ms ~0 on a DNS cache hit is expected. host=standstill-phone (fixed).

title: Towerwatch — 5G Connection Quality
